I agree with the selection of option **B**, *Fibroblasts*, because while some sources might list cementoblasts or osteoblasts in proximity to the periodontium, only fibroblasts are truly representative of the predominant connective tissue cells in the periodontal ligament — accounting for over 90% of cellular content and being responsible for synthesizing key structural proteins like type I collagen.
